title: USPSTF preventive-screening catalog
description: Baked USPSTF preventive-screening dataset (issue #149) read by lib/preventive-catalog.ts. Curated PUBLIC USPSTF grade A/B recommendations — see scripts/gen-screenings.ts for per-row sourcing. Committed + HUMAN-REVIEWABLE; regenerate with `npm run gen:screenings`. SIMPLIFIED and INFORMATIONAL for personal/household tracking, NOT clinical software or medical advice. `meta.reviewed` dates the whole dataset; risk-gated rows stay inert until the risk input exists.

citation.source: U.S. Preventive Services Task Force (USPSTF) — A and B grade recommendations
citation.note: Public recommendation statements; each row carries its own USPSTF `citation` (summary + grade). Simplified for personal tracking, not clinical guidance.

citation.summary: Screen adults 18+ for hypertension; frequency varies by age and prior readings (annual default used here).

entries.name: Cholesterol (lipid) screening
entries.description: Blood lipid panel to assess cardiovascular risk.

citation.summary: Lipids inform the statin-prevention decision for adults 40–75 with cardiovascular risk factors; broader periodic screening is common practice (every ~5 years used here).

entries.name: Colorectal cancer screening
entries.description: Screening for colorectal cancer (e.g. colonoscopy or stool-based test). Interval depends on the test used.

citation.summary: Screen adults 45–75 for colorectal cancer; 76–85 is individualized. Multiple strategies (colonoscopy ~10y, annual stool-based, etc.).

entries.name: Diabetes / prediabetes screening
entries.description: Blood glucose or A1c screening for type 2 diabetes and prediabetes. USPSTF targets overweight/obese adults.

citation.summary: Screen adults 35–70 who are overweight or obese for prediabetes and type 2 diabetes, about every 3 years.

entries.name: Depression screening
entries.description: Screening for depression (e.g. a PHQ-2 / PHQ-9 questionnaire) in adolescents and adults.

citation.summary: Screen adolescents 12–18 and adults 18+ (including during pregnancy and postpartum) for depression where follow-up care is available. Optimal rescreening interval is not established (annual default used here).

entries.name: Anxiety screening
entries.description: Screening for anxiety disorders (e.g. a GAD-7 questionnaire) in children, adolescents, and adults.

citation.summary: Screen children and adolescents 8–18 (2022) and adults 19–64, including during pregnancy and postpartum (2023), for anxiety disorders. Optimal rescreening interval is not established (annual default used here).

entries.name: Alcohol use screening
entries.description: Screening for unhealthy alcohol use (e.g. an AUDIT-C / AUDIT questionnaire) in adults.

citation.summary: Screen adults 18+ (including pregnant women) for unhealthy alcohol use and provide brief behavioral counseling where indicated. Optimal rescreening interval is not established (annual default used here).

entries.name: Drug use screening
entries.description: Screening for unhealthy drug use (e.g. a DAST questionnaire) in adults, by asking about use.

citation.summary: Screen adults 18+ for unhealthy drug use by asking questions about use (2020), when services for accurate diagnosis, treatment, and care can be offered. Optimal rescreening interval is not established (annual default used here).

entries.name: Hepatitis C screening
entries.description: One-time blood test for hepatitis C infection.

citation.summary: Screen adults 18–79 for hepatitis C infection at least once.

entries.name: HIV screening
entries.description: One-time blood test for HIV infection.

citation.summary: Screen adolescents and adults 15–65 for HIV infection at least once (younger adolescents and older adults at increased risk should also be screened).

entries.name: Cervical cancer screening
entries.description: Cervical cancer screening (Pap cytology and/or HPV testing). Interval depends on the test used.

citation.summary: Screen women 21–65: cytology every 3 years (21–65), or HPV / co-testing every 5 years (30–65).

entries.name: Mammography (breast cancer screening)
entries.description: Breast cancer screening with mammography, typically every 2 years.

citation.summary: Screen women 65+ for osteoporosis with bone measurement testing. The optimal rescreening interval is uncertain.

entries.name: Hepatitis B screening
entries.description: Blood test for hepatitis B infection in adolescents and adults at increased risk. Requires risk information — inactive until that is recorded.

citation.summary: Screen adolescents and adults at increased risk for hepatitis B virus infection (2020). The population is risk-defined rather than universal.

entries.name: Lung cancer screening (low-dose CT)
entries.description: Annual low-dose CT for adults 50–80 with a significant smoking history. Requires smoking history — inactive until that is recorded.

citation.summary: Annual low-dose CT for adults 50–80 with a 20 pack-year history who currently smoke or quit within 15 years.

entries.name: Abdominal aortic aneurysm screening
entries.description: One-time ultrasound for men 65–75 who have ever smoked. Requires smoking history — inactive until that is recorded.

citation.summary: One-time abdominal ultrasound for men 65–75 who have ever smoked.
